seconds_v2.desc: Top-down view of multiple mason-jar candles set on a granite countertop; each jar holds a pool of warm cream-colored wax with a wooden wick standing upright in the center.

Mason jars in rows still cooling; wax appears slightly more opaque as it begins to set around the wooden wick tabs.
Camera holds on the same jars; wax surfaces look uniformly creamy, wooden wicks coated by a thin film of solidifying wax.
Closer view of the front jars; small sinkholes start appearing near the wick where wax is contracting around the wooden wick base.
Wax continues to settle; tiny depressions visible at the wick base in the front two jars as wax shrinks and adheres to the wood.
Same shot; the front jars' surfaces dimpled around each wick where wax has pulled in slightly during cooling.
Sinkholes near wicks become more defined in the foreground jars; rest of the surface still creamy and level.
Final frame on the array of cooling mason-jar candles; wax now mostly opaque, wicks firmly seated with small wells forming around each wooden wick tab.

The skewer is withdrawn, revealing a small dark sinkhole opening in the white wax next to the wick.
Close view of the cured candle top now showing the poked sinkhole hole beside the upright wick.
